pattern: Because records arrive with strictly increasing times, appending to a parallel prefix-sum array turns each range total into two binary searches plus a subtraction.
hints: Records come in time order, so the list of times stays sorted as you append.
Keep a running prefix sum alongside the times so any suffix or window sum is a difference of two prefixes.
For totalScore, binary-search the first time >= startTime and the first time > endTime, then subtract the two prefix sums.

edge_inputs.description: query window entirely before any record returns zero
multiple records summed over a wide range
boundaries are inclusive on both ends

stress.description: many records with strictly increasing times and random range queries
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    ops = ['ExamTracker']
    args = [[]]
    t = 0
    times = []
    n = max(1, size)
    # first call must be record
    t += rng.randint(1, 5)
    ops.append('record'); args.append([t, rng.randint(1, 1000)]); times.append(t)
    for _ in range(n):
        if rng.random() < 0.5:
            t += rng.randint(1, 5)
            ops.append('record'); args.append([t, rng.randint(1, 1000)]); times.append(t)
        else:
            a = rng.randint(1, t)
            b = rng.randint(a, t)
            ops.append('totalScore'); args.append([a, b])
    return [ops, args]
stress.note: times strictly increase; queries never exceed the latest recorded time
